method: multi-agent verify + adversarial falsification pass (fresh sub-agent contexts); verifier re-fetched Google's primary announcement and cross-checked vendor benchmark numbers against Artificial Analysis's independent measurement; falsifier re-fetched the primary source, checked for corrections/errors, and confirmed the Bloomberg-sourced 3.5 Pro delay detail across multiple relaying outlets
origin_items: data/ledger/run-report-2026-07-22-2232Z.md — cycle 1 Tier-0 sweep candidate (Google DeepMind Blog)

claims.text: Google introduced three new Gemini models on 21 July 2026: Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber
claims.reasoning: Confirmed against Google's own blog post by both verifier and falsifier, and independently reported by TechCrunch, 9to5Google, MarkTechPost, The Hacker News.

claims.text: 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite are available immediately; 3.5 Flash Cyber is restricted to a limited-access pilot for governments and trusted partners
claims.reasoning: Verbatim match on primary source, confirmed by both agents.

claims.text: Pricing: 3.6 Flash $1.50/$7.50 per million input/output tokens; 3.5 Flash-Lite $0.30/$2.50; Flash Cyber pricing undisclosed
claims.reasoning: Confirmed on primary source and independently by OpenRouter, Artificial Analysis, and other pricing trackers per falsifier check.

claims.text: Google's own benchmark comparisons (MLE-Bench, OSWorld-Verified, output-token reduction) versus 3.5 Flash
claims.reasoning: Vendor-reported figures, transcribed accurately from the primary source but not independently reproduced task-by-task; labeled vendor-claim per policy.

claims.text: Independent Artificial Analysis measurement: flat Intelligence Index score, >50% time-per-task reduction, ~18% cost-per-task reduction
claims.reasoning: Independent evaluator directly measured the release; both verifier and falsifier confirmed these figures against Artificial Analysis's own published article, fetched live this cycle.

claims.text: Multiple outlets, citing Bloomberg, report Gemini 3.5 Pro was held back after missing internal performance targets
claims.reasoning: All relaying outlets (TechCrunch, The Information, PCWorld, Yahoo Finance) trace to one Bloomberg report per the independence rule (verification.md §2); presented in the article as an attributed, unconfirmed report, not stated as our own confirmed fact.

notes: Falsifier flagged two tightenings applied in the published article: (1) the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index result is a plateau, not an improvement, so the article does not imply capability gains where only efficiency gains are supported; (2) the 3.5 Pro delay is presented as an attributed single-source report. No factual errors or corrections found. Dedup-check clean (no archive match) before opening.
